sources.label: NEXTDC Contracted Utilisation and Capex Guidance Update (Apr 2026, pre-window)
Stocks Down Under - NEXTDC record 667MW contracted & A$2.2B capital plan (Apr 2026, pre-window)
ASX market announcements - NXT (no new filings found Jun 6-9)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's move up already captured the April contracted-utilisation surge (667MW, S4 71% contracted) and capex raise; searches found no new ASX filings or material news since June 6, and today's -0.44% is noise, so no basis to move again on the physical-deployment alpha list.
verdicts.bull: Record forward order book (667MW contracted, +60% since Dec 2025) with S4 Sydney filling fast gives multi-year revenue visibility, and the A$2.2B capital plan plus raised FY26 capex (A$2.7-3.0B) shows demand is outrunning supply in Australian AI data-center capacity.
verdicts.neutral: At +26% YTD and an $8B cap, the April evidence is substantially priced; the stock drifts until the next contracted-utilisation update or an S4 anchor-tenant disclosure resets the trajectory.
verdicts.bear: Heavy equity-and-debt-funded capex into a pre-EBITDA-positive hyperscale buildout leaves the name exposed to dilution, cost overruns, and any hyperscaler pause in Australian capacity commitments.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Next contracted-utilisation / forward order book update, particularly further S4 Sydney contracting toward full 350MW
verdicts.primaryRisk: Funding strain or dilution if the A$2.7-3.0B capex program runs ahead of new contracted megawatts
